versionNotes: Sprint 3 demo — partner entry + QFT discussion for information diet and futures thinking
title: Sprint 3 Demo: AI-Facilitated Peer Discussion
description: Your partner has written their Sprint 3 reflection on paper. Enter their responses below, then formulate follow-up questions and discuss them face-to-face.

questions.prompt: Name one specific thing you believe differently about your focus area now compared to Week 9. What did you read, watch, or listen to that caused the shift?
questions.partnerInstructions: Read your partner's written response carefully, then type it below as accurately as you can. Don't paraphrase — capture their actual words.
questions.placeholder: Type your partner's written response here...

questions.prompt: Choose 1 of 2 questions below, then enter your partner's response:
questions.selectorLabel: Which question did your partner respond to?
questionOptions: Describe a source you found during Sprint 3 that you plan to keep consuming after this course ends. What makes it worth your time when most sources aren't?
What pattern or signal did you spot across multiple sources that no single source stated explicitly? Walk through the pieces that led you to see it.
questions.partnerInstructions: First select which question the author chose, then type their written response below.
questions.placeholder: Type your partner's response to their chosen question here...

questions.prompt: Based on your partner's responses above, write 3-4 follow-up questions you want to ask them face-to-face. Use the QFT rules below — tag each question as Open or Closed.
questions.aiContext: This is a Sprint 3 demo discussion in CST349 (Professional Seminar). Sprint 3 focused on information diet — students audited what they actually consume, found new sources, and formed opinions about the future of their field. The student being discussed should have evidence of genuine belief change driven by specific sources, not vague claims about 'reading more.' Look for specificity of sources, depth of the belief shift, and whether the student can trace the change to particular content.

questions.prompt: After this conversation with your partner, what's one thing about their Sprint 3 experience that made you rethink something about your own approach to reading the future of your field?
questions.placeholder: Reflect on the full conversation you just had...
